hypothesis: top-two-layer dynamic routing may stay useful as long as the router is constrained to only two source states
notes: 1821 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.97 MB, valid; the cleaner top-two two-source version also beat the old hybrid anchor

hypothesis: coarse shared routing may be enough at the top of the stack
notes: 1797 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.95 MB, valid; shared-scalar top-only routing lost clearly, so the revived family still wants token-wise flexibility

hypothesis: the final layer may benefit from a richer three-source top-only router rather than the cleaner two-source form
notes: 1853 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.99 MB, valid; the richer final-layer router gave back the small gain, so extra sources were not helpful even at the top

hypothesis: top-only dynamic routing may stack with the clean shared skip-gate redesign
notes: 1820 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.95 MB, valid; top-two-layer AttnRes-lite plus shared skip gates became the second-best run in the whole queue and proved the narrow routing line is real

hypothesis: the current relu^2 MLP should remain strong when surfaced through the new configurable MLP path
notes: 1906 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.05 MB, valid; relu^2 still matched the frontier class under the new MLP implementation surface

hypothesis: plain ReLU may be enough once the backbone is stronger and more routing-efficient
notes: 1908 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.11 MB, valid; dropping the square hurt clearly, so the current MLP really is using the stronger nonlinearity

hypothesis: a smooth SiLU MLP may shape features better than relu^2 on the hybrid frontier
notes: 1905 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.10 MB, valid; SiLU underperformed clearly, so smoothness alone is not the missing ingredient

hypothesis: GELU may fit the small-model regime better than a hard-threshold squared activation
notes: 1904 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.10 MB, valid; GELU also lost clearly, so the broad MLP family stays on the relu^2 side

hypothesis: a gated SiLU MLP may beat all simple activation families if gating is the real missing ingredient
notes: 1643 steps in 600.1s; artifact 19.06 MB, invalid; the gated SiLU MLP was competitive on bpb but broke the size cap, so gating remains a possible later branch

hypothesis: relu^2 remains the right local anchor inside the polynomial-family tranche
notes: 1907 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.04 MB, valid; the polynomial tranche anchor exactly reproduced the current best backbone under the new MLP-mode surface

hypothesis: a stronger cubic emphasis may improve feature expressivity beyond relu^2

hypothesis: the model may want both gentle linear behavior and quadratic amplification inside the MLP
notes: 1908 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.06 MB, valid; mixing linear and quadratic terms produced the best non-anchor MLP result and stayed very close to the frontier

hypothesis: a small cubic correction on top of relu^2 may help without the full instability of relu^3
notes: 1907 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.98 MB, valid; adding a cubic tail still hurt clearly, so cubic structure does not look useful here

hypothesis: polynomial behavior may work better if the hidden path is normalized before squaring
